---
title: "Curriculum Vitae [<i class='bi bi-download'></i>](Wassim_Kabalan_CV.pdf)"
description: "Wassim Kabalan — R&D Engineer & PhD Candidate (APC, CNRS/IN2P3)"
page-layout: article
toc: true
toc-title: "Contents"
number-sections: false
format:
  html:
    css:
      - ../assets/cv.css
---

# Wassim Kabalan

**PhD Candidate & Software Engineer — Computational Cosmology**

I am a PhD candidate and software engineer specializing in computational physics, high-dimensional Bayesian inference, and differentiable programming. I develop **JAX**-based,differentiable and distributed statistical models to extract physical parameters from complex cosmological data.


[![GitHub - ASKabalan](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-ASKabalan-lightblue?style=flat&logo=github&logoColor=black)](https://github.com/ASKabalan/)
[![LinkedIn - Wassim Kabalan](https://img.shields.io/badge/LinkedIn-Wassim%20Kabalan-0A66C2?style=flat&logo=linkedin&logoColor=white)](https://www.linkedin.com/in/wassimkabalan/)
[![Email - wassim@apc.in2p3.fr](https://img.shields.io/badge/Email-wassim%40apc.in2p3.fr-blue?style=flat)](mailto:wassim@apc.in2p3.fr)

---


## Education & Professional Experience

<table class="cv-experience">
<tr>
  <td class="cv-date">Dec 2023 –<br>Dec 2026<br>(expected)</td>
  <td class="cv-content">
    <strong>Graduate Researcher (PhD, Physics of the Universe)</strong>, <em>Université Paris Cité — APC (CNRS/IN2P3)</em>, Paris, France<br>
    <em>Thesis:</em> Automatically differentiable and distributed probabilistic programming for weak-lensing inference.<br>
    Advisors: Eric Aubourg, Alexandre Boucaud, Josquin Errard, François Lanusse.
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Core of the thesis:</strong> developing <strong>JAX</strong>-based, GPU-accelerated, fully differentiable, high-dimensional statistical models to extract physical parameters from cosmological data.</li>
      <li><em>Weak-lensing field-level inference (LSST).</em> Building differentiable cosmological simulations as forward models, then running MCMC chains over a ~10⁹-dimensional Bayesian posterior on up to <strong>512 H100 GPUs</strong> to recover cosmological parameters directly from large-scale-structure data.</li>
      <li><em>CMB component separation (Simons Observatory).</em> Built a robust GPU-accelerated optimization framework, and led the design of a spatial clustering strategy for upcoming CMB satellite missions; first author of the corresponding paper (<a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08463">arXiv:2604.08463</a>).</li>
    </ul>
  </td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Apr 2023 –<br>May 2023</td>
<td class="cv-content">**Advanced AI for Data Analysis**, *École Polytechnique (Executive Education)*, Palaiseau</td>
</tr>

<tr>
  <td class="cv-date">Oct 2019 –<br>Oct 2023</td>
  <td class="cv-content">
    <strong>3DEXPERIENCE & CATIA Software Infrastructure Engineer</strong>, <em>Dassault Systèmes</em>, Vélizy-Villacoublay, France
    <ul>
      <li>Developed features and maintained core infrastructure inside a <strong>multi-million-line C++ codebase</strong> shared by hundreds of engineers across teams.</li>
      <li>Built and extended core conversion services and the CATIA cache infrastructure in modern <strong>C++ (17/20)</strong> on <strong>Linux</strong>, including a <em>multi-session distributed cache</em>.</li>
      <li>Built <strong>CI/CD</strong> pipelines and a test matrix for the conversion service infrastructure.</li>
    </ul>
  </td>
</tr>

<tr>
  <td class="cv-date">Jan 2019 –<br>Oct 2019</td>
  <td class="cv-content">
    <strong>Data Acquisition Engineer</strong>, <em>SERMA (for Renault)</em>, Guyancourt, France
    <ul>
      <li>Built tooling for high-volume vehicle sensor logs, including a custom <strong>C</strong> decoder for ECU binary data.</li>
      <li>Developed parallel <strong>Python</strong> post-processing workflows on an <strong>88-core</strong> server.</li>
      <li>Automated end-to-end conversion pipeline for vehicle network data.</li>
    </ul>
  </td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Sep 2016 –<br>Sep 2018</td>
<td class="cv-content">**M2, Electronics, Electrical & Automation**, *Université Gustave Eiffel*</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Sep 2013 –<br>Nov 2016</td>
<td class="cv-content">**Licence, Engineering Science**, *Université Paris-Est Créteil (UPEC)*</td>
</tr>
</table>

---

## Collaborations

- **LSST Dark Energy Science Collaboration (DESC)** — Member; *weak-lensing* forward models and scalable, differentiable simulation tools (`JaxPM`, `jax-fli`).
- **Simons Observatory** — Member; component separation tooling (`FURAX`) for *CMB* analysis.

---

## Open-Source Software

- [`jax-fli`](https://github.com/ASKabalan/jax-fli) — **Author**. **JAX**-based *field-level inference* package; fully distributed, high-resolution N-body and lensing simulations.
- [`jaxDecomp`](https://github.com/DifferentiableUniverseInitiative/jaxDecomp) — **Author / Maintainer**. **JAX** library for distributed 3D domain decomposition and FFTs: **NVIDIA cuDecomp** bindings (**multi-GPU**, **NCCL**) plus a pure-**JAX** backend for portability across CUDA / TPU / CPU.
- [`JaxPM`](https://github.com/DifferentiableUniverseInitiative/JaxPM) — **Main contributor / Maintainer**. Differentiable particle-mesh simulations in **JAX** with multi-accelerator support; used for *weak-lensing* forward modeling.
- [`jax-healpy`](https://github.com/CMBSciPol/jax-healpy) — **Main contributor / Maintainer**. **JAX**-native HEALPix utilities; AD-friendly, GPU-ready.
- [`FURAX`](https://github.com/CMBSciPol/furax) — **Main contributor / Maintainer**. **JAX** framework for *inverse problems*; used for Simons Observatory *CMB component separation*.

---

## Talks & Tutorials

- **Generative AI with JAX** [Tutorial] • AISSAI School 2025 • Oct 2025 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2025_10_AI)
- **JAXPM: Scalable and Differentiable Particle-Mesh Simulations** • Bayesian Deep Learning Workshop • May 2025 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2025_05_BDL/index.html#/title-slide)
- **Bayesian Inference for Cosmology with JAX** [Tutorial] • Bayesian Deep Learning Workshop • May 2025 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2025_05_BDL/index.html#/title-slide)
- **Massively Parallel Computing in Cosmology with JAX** [Tutorial] • CoPhy 2024 • Nov 2024 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2024_11_CoPhy/index.html#/title-slide)
- **Differentiable and Distributed Particle-Mesh N-body Simulations** • LSST France 2024 • Jun 2024 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2024_06_LSST_FR/index.html#/title-slide)

---

## Publications

**Refereed**

- Spagnoletti, A., Boucaud, A., Huertas-Company, M., **Kabalan, W.**, and Biswas, B. 2024. [Bayesian Deconvolution of Astronomical Images with Diffusion Models: Quantifying Prior-Driven Features in Reconstructions](https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.19158). arXiv:2411.19158 [astro-ph.IM].
  *Contribution:* **JAX**-based deconvolution code, multi-node simulations on Jean Zay HPC.

- Sommer, K., **Kabalan, W.**, and Brunet, R. 2024. [Infrared Radiometric Image Classification and Segmentation of Cloud Structure Using Deep-learning Framework for Ground-based Infrared Thermal Camera Observations](https://egusphere.copernicus.org/preprints/2024/egusphere-2024-101/). EGUsphere Preprint 2024-101.
  *Contribution:* **JAX**-based U-Net implementation and training on Jean Zay HPC. Code: [github.com/ASKabalan/infrared-cloud-detection](https://github.com/ASKabalan/infrared-cloud-detection)

**Preprints**

- **Kabalan, W.**, Rizzieri, A., Sohn, W., Basyrov, A., Boucaud, A., Beringue, B., Chanial, P., Tsang King Sang, E., and Errard, J. 2026. [A GPU-Accelerated JAX Framework for Robust Parametric Component Separation and Clustering Optimization for CMB Polarization Satellites](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08463). arXiv:2604.08463 [astro-ph.CO].

**Software**

- **Kabalan, W.**, Lanusse, F., Boucaud, A., and Aubourg, E. 2025. jaxDecomp: JAX Library for 3D Domain Decomposition and Parallel FFTs. Submitted to JOSS.

**In Preparation**

- **Kabalan, W.**, Lanusse, F., Boucaud, A., and Aubourg, E. 2026. JAXPM: A JAX-Based Framework for Scalable and Differentiable Particle Mesh Simulations.

---

## Supervision

<table class="cv-experience">
<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Jan 2025 –<br>Jun 2025</td>
<td class="cv-content">
**Binh Nguyen**, Master 1 Student<br>
*Project:* Inference of Weak-Lensing Parameters from Blended Galaxies Using Generative Neural Networks. [Final report](https://binhola.github.io/littlewolfz/M2-NPAC/reports/VAE_deblender_final_report_M1_GP_2025.pdf).<br>
*Outcome:* Talk at Rencontres du Vietnam.
</td>
</tr>
</table>

---

## Skills

**Programming**
**Python** (7y), **JAX** (3y), **C++** (5y), **CUDA** (3y), **PyTorch** (1y)

**HPC & GPU Computing**
**NCCL**, **MPI**, Slurm, Nsight; **multi-node GPU** (up to **512 H100s**), distributed FFTs, custom CUDA primitives via XLA FFI/CustomCall

**Statistics & Machine Learning**
High-dimensional Bayesian inference (MCMC, HMC, NUTS) with NumPyro / BlackJAX; simulation-based and *field-level inference*; generative deep learning; uncertainty quantification

**DevOps & Software Engineering**
GitHub/GitLab CI, packaging (PyPI), containers, Linux, CMake, TDD

---

## Languages

<style>
  /* compact table rows */
  table.cv-experience.compact td {
    padding: 2px 8px;     /* was likely ~8–12px */
    line-height: 1.15;    /* tighter line height */
    vertical-align: top;
  }
  table.cv-experience.compact .cv-date {
    width: 120px;         /* tweak as you like */
    white-space: nowrap;
  }
</style>

<table class="cv-experience compact">
  <tr>
    <td class="cv-date">French</td>
    <td class="cv-content">Native</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="cv-date">English</td>
    <td class="cv-content">Professional proficiency (C1)</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="cv-date">Arabic</td>
    <td class="cv-content">Native</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="cv-date">German</td>
    <td class="cv-content">Basic (A2)</td>
  </tr>
</table>


---
